svg.title: TWP harm network: tire wear particle causal pathways — light mode
svg.desc: Six-tier node diagram showing how tire wear particle emissions cascade through transport, exposure, harm, economic, and systemic layers.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about TWP emissions and PM10 vs PM2.5 in California')
sendPrompt('How does PM10 settle on farmland within 1–2km of California highways?')
sendPrompt('How far does PM2.5 from tire wear travel and who does it reach?')
sendPrompt('What crops are most at risk from tire wear particle deposition in the San Joaquin valley?')
sendPrompt('How do tire wear particles accumulate in agricultural soil over time?')
sendPrompt('What is the respiratory risk for farmworkers from PM2.5 tire wear particles?')
sendPrompt('How do tire wear particles reduce crop yields and affect food safety?')
sendPrompt('What is the toxicity mechanism of 6PPD-quinone in soil and water runoff?')
sendPrompt('What respiratory conditions are linked to chronic PM2.5 exposure in farmworker populations?')
sendPrompt('How was the $45.1B agricultural value at risk calculated in the TWP analysis?')
sendPrompt('What are the estimated remediation costs for tire wear particle soil contamination?')
sendPrompt('What is the estimated healthcare cost burden from PM2.5 farmworker exposure in California?')
sendPrompt('How does tire wear particle crop contamination create systemic food supply chain risk?')
sendPrompt('How does highway proximity create disproportionate environmental justice risk for farmworker communities?')
sendPrompt('What insurance and liability implications exist for landowners and state DOTs from tire wear particle emissions?')
sendPrompt('Explain how the COVID lockdown validated the TWP emissions model with a 10.1% drop in emissions')
